page: Amazon Web Services r8gb.48xlarge is a memory-optimized server instance powered by the AWS Graviton processor family. Built on the arm64 architecture, this instance features 192 dedicated physical cores running at 2.7 GHz using the ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 microarchitecture. It is equipped with 1536.0 GB of RAM, providing a memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1 data cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance operates on the AWS Nitro hypervisor and features a baseline network bandwidth of 200 Gbps. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark data highlights the instance's strong multi-threaded capabilities and memory subsystem performance. In multi-core evaluations, the server achieves top-tier results in stress-ng multi-core CPU performance, bzip3 multi-threaded decompression, and PassMark CPU physics tests. Its memory subsystem demonstrates top-tier read and write bandwidth across both small and large blocks, alongside a top-tier PassMark memory score. However, single-core performance is more moderate, with stress-ng single-core, Geekbench single-core, and gzip compression single-threaded benchmarks placing in the average tier. Image processing performance is a notable bottleneck, registering in the weak tier.

Given its massive memory capacity and high-density multi-core architecture, the r8gb.48xlarge is highly cost-efficient for workloads requiring substantial resource density without the overhead of simultaneous multithreading, as each of its 192 vCPUs maps to a dedicated physical core. Top-tier performance in PassMark database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput indicates a strong fit for large-scale in-memory databases, key-value stores, and high-throughput web serving. Additionally, strong multi-core compiling performance makes it suitable for large-scale software build systems, while its moderate LLM prompt processing speeds for medium and large models support CPU-based inference tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services r8gb.48xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 192 dedicated physical cores on the arm64 ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 architecture. Operating at 2.7 GHz on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 1536.0 GB of RAM, yielding 8.0 GB of memory per core, alongside a 200 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The instance does not include local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmark results show top-tier multi-core CPU performance and memory bandwidth, though single-core metrics and image processing speeds remain average to weak. The server is highly efficient for high-density, memory-intensive workloads. It is optimized for large-scale in-memory databases, Redis deployments, high-throughput static web serving, software compilation, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.

tagline: A high-density, memory-optimized arm64 platform featuring 192 dedicated physical cores and 1.5 TB of RAM for demanding database workloads.
